# Tadori Evidence Baseline

Purpose: single verified evidence pack for downstream planning subagents.
Every claim below carries a path, and code claims carry line numbers. This
document does not propose changes; see the explicitly labeled
"PROPOSED / UNVERIFIED" subsections for anything speculative. Generated by
reading the repository directly on 2026-07-17; no production files were
modified to produce it.

---

### `tsconfig.json` summary (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\tsconfig.json`)

- Extends `./tsconfig.base.json`.
- `include`: `packages/{core,store,indexer,harness,mcp}/{src,test}/**/*.ts`,
  `vitest.config.ts`, `eslint.config.js`.
- `exclude`: `node_modules`, `packages/fixtures`.

### `tsconfig.base.json` summary (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\tsconfig.base.json`)

- `target`/`module`/`moduleResolution`: `ES2022` / `NodeNext` / `NodeNext`.
- `strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`, `noImplicitOverride: true`,
  `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch: true`, `isolatedModules: true`,
  `esModuleInterop: true`, `resolveJsonModule: true`, `skipLibCheck: true`,
  `noEmit: true`.
- **No `allowJs` and no `checkJs` key at all** (verified: absent from the
  file). This is load-bearing for Section 4 below.
- `paths` maps all five `@tadori/*` package names to their
  `packages/<name>/src/index.ts`.

### ESLint config file name

`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\eslint.config.js` (flat config, `tseslint.config(...)`
wrapping `js.configs.recommended` + `tseslint.configs.recommended`). Ignores
`node_modules/**`, `packages/fixtures/**`, `docs/**`, `**/dist/**`,
`**/coverage/**`, `.tmp/**`. Rules of note: `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any:
error`, `no-console: off`, `eqeqeq: always`. No `allowJs`/`checkJs` setting
present (ESLint has no such option; irrelevant to the TS compiler gate, noted
only for completeness).

## 3. Package public APIs

All five `src/index.ts` files are pure re-export barrels
(`export * from "./x.js"`); the tables below list every named export reachable
through that barrel, resolved to its defining file.

#### Migration files (001–006), read from `packages/store/src/migrations.ts`

All six migrations live in **one file**, not six separate files (the task
description's "001–006" phrasing refers to logical migration objects
`migration001`..`migration006`, each a `{version, name, sql}` constant). File
header (lines 1-6) states migrations 1-5 are copied verbatim from "Tadori
v2.1 - Frozen Implementation Corrections §9" and must not be edited; later
migrations require a proven defect cited in `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`.

| Version | Name | Tables created | Indexes | Triggers |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 (`migrations.ts:14-193`) | repositories, snapshots, stable entities, memberships, evidence | `schema_migrations`, `repositories`, `repository_snapshots`, `file_entities`, `snapshot_files`, `node_entities`, `snapshot_nodes`, `edge_entities`, `snapshot_edges`, `evidence_items`, `node_evidence`, `edge_evidence` | `idx_snapshots_repo_created`, `idx_snapshot_files_path`, `idx_snapshot_files_package`, `idx_node_entities_name`, `idx_node_entities_kind`, `idx_snapshot_nodes_file`, `idx_snapshot_nodes_lines`, `idx_edge_entities_src`, `idx_edge_entities_dst`, `idx_snapshot_edges_snapshot`, `idx_snapshot_edges_origin` | none |
| 2 (`migrations.ts:195-284`) | boundaries and explicit decisions | `boundary_entities`, `snapshot_boundaries`, `boundary_evidence`, `decision_entities`, `snapshot_decisions`, `decision_evidence`, `decision_links` | `idx_decision_link_node`, `idx_decision_link_edge`, `idx_decision_link_boundary` (all partial unique indexes) | none |
| 3 (`migrations.ts:286-433`) | tasks, retrieval, observed events, changes, tests | `tasks`, `retrieval_events`, `retrieval_result_nodes`, `retrieval_result_edges`, `retrieval_omissions`, `agent_events`, `agent_event_targets`, `change_sets`, `change_set_files`, `test_runs`, `test_run_cases` | `idx_tasks_repo_started`, `idx_retrieval_events_task`, `idx_agent_event_target_file`, `idx_agent_event_target_node` | none |
| 4 (`migrations.ts:435-474`) | layouts and quarantined summaries | `layout_positions`, `summaries` | `idx_layout_level` | none |
| 5 (`migrations.ts:476-496`) | full-text search | `node_fts` (virtual, FTS5, `tokenize='unicode61'`) | none (FTS5 self-indexes) | none |
| 6 (`migrations.ts:504-564`) | atomic immutable snapshot activation events | `snapshot_activations` | `idx_snapshot_activations_current`, `idx_snapshot_activations_snapshot` | `snapshot_activations_validate_insert`, `snapshot_activations_validate_update` (both `BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE`, abort on repo/kind mismatch) |

Migration 6 comment (`migrations.ts:498-503`, verbatim): "Defect correction:
UNIQUE(repo_id, kind, workspace_hash) means a working tree that changes A ->
B -> A cannot publish A again by inserting another snapshot... Immutable
activation events make publication explicit."

---

### `packages/harness` (barrel: `packages/harness/src/index.ts`: `expected.js`, `milestone.js`, `compare.js`, `validateFixtures.js`)

Exports cover: fixture-target discovery (`fixtureSnapshotTargets`, in
`expected.ts`), milestone-relation filtering (`milestone.ts`), fixture
comparison (`compareFixtureSnapshot`, `formatComparison` in `compare.ts`), and
schema/identity/evidence validation (`validateFixtures` in
`validateFixtures.ts`). Consumed directly by the three harness CLIs (§6/§7).

---

### `packages/mcp` (barrel: `packages/mcp/src/index.ts`: `contracts.js`, `concurrentRefresh.js`, `events.js`, `ranking.js`, `server.js`, `service.js`, `stdio.js`, `tools.js`)

**Six tool names** (`TOOL_NAMES`, `packages/mcp/src/contracts.ts:12-19`,
verbatim):

```ts
export const TOOL_NAMES = [
  "repo_overview",
  "find_symbol",
  "symbol_context",
  "find_tests",
  "impact",
  "path"
] as const;
```

**Snapshot query service**: `GraphService` class,
`packages/mcp/src/service.ts:66-...`. Key surface:

- `static open(db: Database, repoRoot: string, refreshOverlay?: RefreshFreshnessOverlay, preferredKind?: RepoStateKind): GraphService` (`service.ts:120-153`) — resolves the repo row by root path, calls `getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id, preferredKind)` (falling back to `getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id)` with no kind filter if a preferred-kind lookup misses), then `loadSnapshotGraph(db, snapshot.id)`, then `ensureSnapshotFts(db, loaded.snapshot.id)` to repair/rebuild FTS rows before serving.
- Public readonly fields: `snapshot: SnapshotRow`, `repoId: number`, `graph: StoredSnapshotGraph`, `nodesByKey`, plus in-memory adjacency/fan-in indexes built in the constructor (`service.ts:80-118`).
- `fanIn(entityKey)`, `searchNodes(query, limit, kind?, offset?)` (delegates to `@tadori/store`'s `searchNodeFts`), and other resolution/read helpers (not exhaustively enumerated here — see file for the full read-only query surface).
- `estimateTokens(text)` — free function, `service.ts:358`.

**Stdio CLI entry** — `packages/mcp/src/cli.ts` (68 lines total):

- Flags, parsed by `parseCliOptions(argv)` (`cli.ts:10-33`): `--db <path>` (required) and `--repo <path>` (required); both must have a following value or `parseCliOptions` throws; any other argument throws `Unknown argument`; if either flag is missing, throws `"Usage: tadori-mcp --db <sqlite-path> --repo <repository-root>"`.
- `main()` (`cli.ts:35-62`): `openDatabase(options.dbPath)` → `runMigrations(db)` → `startStdioServer({db, repoRoot})` (from `stdio.ts`) → registers `stdin 'end'`, `SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`, `exit` handlers that call `runtime.close(...)` / `runtime.finalize(...)` and `db.close()`.
- `startStdioServer` (`packages/mcp/src/stdio.ts:24-...`) starts a `ConcurrentRefreshController` (isolated compiler/watcher worker), then calls `GraphService.open(options.db, options.repoRoot, refresh, "working_tree")` — i.e., the MCP server always prefers a `working_tree` snapshot kind, falling back per `GraphService.open`'s internal logic.
- **How a snapshot database is opened/selected**: `openDatabase` (store) → `runMigrations` (store) → `GraphService.open` (mcp/service.ts), which internally calls `getActiveSnapshot` and `loadSnapshotGraph` (both store exports). No default `--db` path — the CLI requires it explicit (contrast with `scripts/tadori.mts`, which defaults to `.tadori/tadori.sqlite` — see §7).

---

## 4. Known defect (verbatim record, do not fix)

### `scan.ts` `classify` function (verbatim, `packages/indexer/src/scan.ts:104-124`)

```ts
function classify(normalizedPath: string): Pick<ScannedFile, "indexed" | "language"> {
  const ext = path.posix.extname(normalizedPath).toLowerCase();
  if (normalizedPath.endsWith(".d.ts") || normalizedPath.endsWith(".d.mts") || normalizedPath.endsWith(".d.cts")) {
    // Declaration shims participate in compiler resolution without becoming
    // graph file nodes (golden fixture contract §2).
    return { indexed: false, language: "typescript" };
  }
  if (TS_EXTENSIONS.has(ext)) {
    return { indexed: true, language: "typescript" };
  }
  if (JS_EXTENSIONS.has(ext)) {
    return { indexed: true, language: "javascript" };
  }
  if (ext === ".md") {
    return { indexed: true, language: "markdown" };
  }
  if (ext === ".json") {
    return { indexed: false, language: "json" };
  }
  return { indexed: false, language: "other" };
}
```

`.js`/`.jsx`/`.mjs`/`.cjs` (`JS_EXTENSIONS`, defined at `scan.ts:35`) are
classified `{indexed: true}` **unconditionally** at line 114-116 — no check
of `allowJs`/`checkJs` or any tsconfig state is possible here because `scan.ts`
has no access to compiler options; it only sees a bare repo root path.

### `project.ts` gate 1 — one-shot `createProjectServices` (verbatim, `packages/indexer/src/project.ts:231-241`)

```ts
  const compilerOptions: ts.CompilerOptions = parsed ? parsed.options : { ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS };
  const allowJs = compilerOptions.allowJs === true || compilerOptions.checkJs === true;

  const jsExtensions = new Set([".js", ".jsx", ".mjs", ".cjs"]);
  const programCompatible = (file: string): boolean => {
    const ext = path.extname(file).toLowerCase();
    if (jsExtensions.has(ext)) {
      return allowJs;
    }
    return ext === ".ts" || ext === ".tsx" || ext === ".mts" || ext === ".cts";
  };
```

### `project.ts` gate 2 — persistent `IncrementalProjectServices.computeRootFileNames` (verbatim, `packages/indexer/src/project.ts:354-363`)

```ts
  private computeRootFileNames(scannedSourceAbsolutePaths: readonly string[]): string[] {
    const allowJs =
      this.compilerOptions.allowJs === true || this.compilerOptions.checkJs === true;
    const compatible = (file: string): boolean => {
      const extension = path.extname(file).toLowerCase();
      if ([".js", ".jsx", ".mjs", ".cjs"].includes(extension)) {
        return allowJs;
      }
      return [".ts", ".tsx", ".mts", ".cts"].includes(extension);
    };
```

Both gates independently re-derive the same `allowJs`/`checkJs` boolean and
independently filter out `.js`/`.jsx`/`.mjs`/`.cjs` from **program root
files** when neither compiler option is set. Since `tsconfig.base.json`
(§2 above) sets **neither** `allowJs` nor `checkJs`, and the repo root
`tsconfig.json` (which is what `findTsconfig` in `project.ts:24-32` looks
for — the **root-level** `tsconfig.json`, not `tsconfig.base.json`) also
inherits that same absence via `extends`, any `.js` file scanned as
`indexed: true` by `scan.ts` has **no corresponding program root** in either
`createProjectServices` or `IncrementalProjectServices`. Running
`pnpm tadori diff .` on the Tadori repo itself crashes on
`eslint.config.js` with "Could not find source file" because extraction
(`extract.ts`) attempts to process a file `scan.ts` marked indexed but for
which the TypeScript `Program` has no source file.

### Callers of `scanRepository` (grep, exhaustive)

| Caller | Location |
|---|---|
| `packages/mcp/src/service.ts:264` (inside `GraphService`, used for a workspace-hash/file-listing helper) | imports at `service.ts:5` |
| `packages/indexer/src/indexRepository.ts:84` (`captureRepository`) | imports at `indexRepository.ts:9` |
| `packages/indexer/test/region-merge.test.ts:62` | imports at `region-merge.test.ts:18` |
| `scanRepository` itself is defined at `packages/indexer/src/scan.ts:127` | — |

### Consumers of `ScanResult.indexedFiles` (grep, exhaustive)

| Consumer | Location |
|---|---|
| `mcp/src/service.ts:265` — merges `indexedFiles` + `supportFiles` into a file listing | |
| `indexer/src/indexRepository.ts:37` — `rejectSyntacticallyInvalidRepository` iterates `scan.indexedFiles`, calls `getSyntacticDiagnostics` for ts/js files | |
| `indexer/src/indexRepository.ts:86` — `captureRepository` reads bytes for every indexed+support file | |
| `indexer/src/indexRepository.ts:118` — `indexRepository` filters `scan.indexedFiles` to ts/js, passes absolute paths into `createProjectServices` (this is the exact hand-off point where the gate mismatch bites) | |
| `indexer/src/incremental.ts:142, 187, 651, 659, 727` — multiple uses: computing affected paths, capturedTexts map, full-rescan path lists | |
| `indexer/src/extract.ts:241, 243, 260, 284` — `indexedByPath` map, default region, active-file filtering for extraction | |
| `harness/src/compare.ts:206` — `expectedFiles` sorted list compared against fixture expectations | |
| `indexer/test/region-merge.test.ts:65` | |

**Blast radius**: the defect surfaces wherever `indexRepository`/
`indexRepositoryIntoStore`/`IncrementalRepositoryIndexer` process a repository
containing plain `.js`/`.jsx`/`.mjs`/`.cjs` files without `allowJs`/`checkJs`
set — i.e., every indexing entry point (`indexRepository.ts`,
`incremental.ts`, and transitively `diffWorkingTree.ts`, `scripts/tadori.mts`,
and `mcp` service refresh paths in `service.ts`/`concurrentRefresh.ts`/
`refreshWorker.ts`, none of which special-case this).

### Test coverage of scan classification

No dedicated `scan.test.ts` exists. `packages/indexer/test/region-merge.test.ts`
is the only test file that calls `scanRepository` directly (`region-merge.test.ts:62,65`),
and it does so incidentally to test regional merge behavior, not to assert
classification results for `.js`/`.jsx`/`.mjs`/`.cjs` inputs specifically. No
test file asserts on `classify()`'s return value or on JS-extension handling
by name (grep for `classify` across `packages/indexer/test` returns no
matches other than `region-merge.test.ts`'s incidental use of `scanRepository`).

### Fixture `.js` file check

`find packages/fixtures -iname "*.js" -o -iname "*.jsx" -o -iname "*.mjs" -o
-iname "*.cjs"` returns **zero results**. `fixture-manifest.json`
(`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\fixture-manifest.json`) lists only 4 fixture entries
(`01-core-symbols`, `02-express-routes`, `03-next-routes`,
`04-diff-coalescing`, the last contributing `before`/`after` snapshots — 5
snapshots total, matching README's "all five golden fixtures compare
exactly"); none of their `covers` arrays mention plain JavaScript files. **The
golden fixture corpus contains no JS files at all**, so this defect is
structurally invisible to the fixture-based validation pipeline
(`fixtures:validate`, `fixtures:index`, `fixtures:typecheck`) — it can only be
observed by running the indexer against a real-world repository that itself
contains `.js` files, which is exactly what `pnpm tadori diff .` does against
the Tadori repo itself (which has `eslint.config.js`).

An existing planning artifact already tracks this defect:
`blueprints/INDEX.md` row `00-01A` — "allowJs scanner contract & regression",
Phase 0, status `drafting`, complexity `S`, no dependencies, no impl commit
yet (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\blueprints\INDEX.md:14`). A more detailed
blueprint file `blueprints/00-01A-allowjs-scanner-contract.md` is referenced
in the roadmap phrasing but was not opened/verified in this pass (out of
scope for this evidence pack; flagged here as a pointer only).

## 6. Fixture contract

`fixture-manifest.json` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\fixture-manifest.json`),
`schemaVersion: "1.0.0"`, `root: "packages/fixtures"`:

| Fixture id | Path | Files (total/indexed) | Expected nodes | Expected edges |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `core-symbols` | `packages/fixtures/01-core-symbols` | 15 / 12 | 32 | 72 |
| `express-routes` | `packages/fixtures/02-express-routes` | 17 / 13 | 33 | 79 |
| `next-routes` | `packages/fixtures/03-next-routes` | 13 / 11 | 30 | 68 |
| `diff-coalescing` (before) | `packages/fixtures/04-diff-coalescing` | 8 | 17 | 36 |
| `diff-coalescing` (after) | `packages/fixtures/04-diff-coalescing` | 8 | 17 | 37 |

`diff-coalescing` additionally provides `expected/raw-diff.json` and
`expected/coalesced-diff.json` (not just before/after graphs). Each fixture
directory contains `expected/` and `repo/` subdirectories (`04-` additionally
has `before/`/`after/`).

`schemas/` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\schemas`): `expected-graph.schema.json`,
`expected-diff.schema.json` — JSON Schemas the expected fixture artifacts must
satisfy.

`validate_fixtures.py` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\validate_fixtures.py`, 3994
bytes) — Python reference validator; per README.md line 100, run via
`python validate_fixtures.py` as an independent (non-TypeScript) check of
schema, identity, endpoint, and evidence correctness — a cross-language
sanity check against the same fixture contract the TS harness validates.

Harness CLIs (`packages/harness/src`):
- `cli-validate.ts` — calls `validateFixtures(repoRoot)`; exits 1 and prints errors if any; success message: "Tadori golden fixtures: all schema, identity, endpoint, and evidence checks passed."
- `cli-index.ts` — for each `fixtureSnapshotTargets(repoRoot)`, calls `compareFixtureSnapshot` + `formatComparison`; exits 1 if any comparison fails.
- `cli-typecheck-fixtures.ts` — for each fixture target, spawns `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` in `target.sourceRoot` using the repo's own `node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc`; exits 1 on any non-zero exit code.

## 7. Scripts

### `scripts/tadori.mts` (verbatim flow, 33 lines total)

1. `usage()` throws `"Usage: tadori diff <repository> [--db <sqlite-path>]"` if `args[0] !== "diff"` or `args[1]` missing.
2. `root = path.resolve(args[1])`.
3. `dbPath` defaults to `path.join(root, ".tadori", "tadori.sqlite")`; overridable via `--db <path>` as the sole optional trailing flag (strict: any other/extra argument re-triggers `usage()`).
4. `mkdirSync(path.dirname(dbPath), {recursive: true})`.
5. `db = openDatabase(dbPath)`.
6. `runMigrations(db)`.
7. `const result = await diffWorkingTree(db, root)` (from `@tadori/indexer`, but imported here via relative source path `../packages/indexer/src/index.ts`, not the package name — this script runs pre-build against source).
8. `process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2) + "\n")`.
9. `finally { db.close() }`.

`diffWorkingTree` itself (`packages/indexer/src/diffWorkingTree.ts:24-61`):
resolves `root`; looks up the `repositories` row by normalized root path;
computes `base` = `getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id, "working_tree")` falling
back to `getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id, "commit")`; constructs a fresh
`IncrementalRepositoryIndexer(db, root)`, calls `initialize()` then
`waitForIdle()`; reads `indexer.state()` for the new `snapshotId`; calls
`diffSnapshotEdges(db, baseSnapshotId, state.snapshotId)`, sorts
deterministically; always `finally { await indexer.stop() }`. `initialize()`
is exactly the path that calls `indexRepositoryIntoStore` →
`indexRepository` → `captureRepository` → `scanRepository` and
`createProjectServices`, i.e., the exact call chain that hits the §4 defect
when the target repository contains bare `.js` files without `allowJs`.

### `scripts/benchmark-incremental.mts` (192 lines)

Corpus: `FILE_COUNT = 250` leaf files × `LINES_PER_FILE = 1_000` lines each,
plus a `CHAIN_COUNT = 40` import-chain, `ITERATIONS = 12` (constants at
lines 15-18) — matches `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`'s documented "250,330 LOC
in 291 files, 12 incremental iterations" corpus. Gates enforced via thrown
errors (`benchmark-incremental.mts:174-186`):

- `singleFile.p95Ms >= 2_000` → throws (`< 2000ms` gate).
- Package-invalidation duration `>= 10_000` → throws (`< 10000ms` gate).
- Heap growth exceeding a documented 512 MiB ceiling → throws.
- Database growth exceeding a documented 2 MiB-per-snapshot ceiling → throws.

### `scripts/sync-skills.mjs` / `scripts/check-skills.mjs`

- `sync-skills.mjs` (59 lines) — copies the 4 canonical skill directories (`tadori-spec-guardian`, `tadori-validation`, `tadori-indexer`, `tadori-visual-workflow`) into `.claude/` and `.agents/`, using `frontmatterProblems` from `./skill-frontmatter.mjs` to validate frontmatter first.
- `check-skills.mjs` (46 lines) — verifies the synced copies in `.claude/`/`.agents/` are byte-identical to the canonical source, same 4 skill names, same frontmatter validator.

---

## 8. Extension points for Phase 7+ (facts only)

**How a read-only server could open the store DB** (verified call chain, no
new code): `openDatabase(path)` (`packages/store/src/database.ts:18`) →
`runMigrations(db)` (`database.ts:44`, idempotent, safe to call even if
already migrated) → `GraphService.open(db, repoRoot, refreshOverlay?,
preferredKind?)` (`packages/mcp/src/service.ts:120-153`). Internally `open`
calls:
- `getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id, preferredKind)` (`store/src/snapshots.ts:626`) — selects the active snapshot for a repo/kind, validated against dangling endpoints.
- `loadSnapshotGraph(db, snapshot.id)` (`store/src/snapshots.ts:696`) — full node/edge/file/evidence reconstruction.
- `ensureSnapshotFts(db, snapshot.id)` (`store/src/search.ts:41`) — repairs FTS5 rows if stale, itself read-safe (only writes if integrity check finds a mismatch).
- `searchNodeFts(db, snapshotId, query, limit, kind?, offset?)` (`store/src/search.ts:83`) is available directly on the same `db` handle for full-text search independent of `GraphService`.
- `listSnapshots(db, repoId)` (`store/src/snapshots.ts:616`) and `getSnapshot(db, snapshotId)` (`store/src/snapshots.ts:610`) are the two other read-only snapshot-listing primitives; neither is currently called by `GraphService` but both are exported from the package barrel.

**Where `.tadori/tadori.sqlite` is created**: only `scripts/tadori.mts:15`
(`path.join(root, ".tadori", "tadori.sqlite")` as the default, overridable
with `--db`). The `mcp:stdio` script (`packages/mcp/src/cli.ts`) has **no
default** — `--db` is a required flag there; README.md's documented usage
(line 49) happens to pass the same `.tadori/tadori.sqlite` path by
convention, not by code default. `.gitignore` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\.gitignore`)
was modified this session per the git-status snapshot but its content was
not re-verified in this pass beyond noting `.tadori/` exists as a live
directory at repo root (`ls` at task start showed `drwxr-xr-x ... .tadori`).

**How the MCP layer's query service could be reused by an HTTP server**:
`GraphService` (`packages/mcp/src/service.ts`) has no dependency on the MCP
stdio transport (`stdio.ts`) or the `TadoriTools` wrapper (`tools.ts`) to
construct or query — `GraphService.open(db, repoRoot, ...)` is a standalone
static factory over a plain `better-sqlite3` `Database` handle. `TadoriTools`
(`packages/mcp/src/tools.ts:28`, `export class TadoriTools`) wraps a
`GraphService` plus an `EventLog` (`events.ts`) to implement the six
frozen-schema tool contracts (`contracts.ts`); the same `GraphService`
instance could in principle back an HTTP handler without invoking anything
MCP-protocol-specific, since `stdio.ts`'s only MCP-specific pieces are
`StdioServerTransport` and `createTadoriMcpServer` (`server.ts`), both
downstream of, not required by, `GraphService`.

**What does NOT exist yet** — verified via `ls packages/`:

```
core
fixtures
harness
indexer
mcp
store
```

No `packages/server`, `packages/cli`, `apps/viz`, `packages/hooks`,
`packages/bench` directories exist anywhere in the repository. This matches
README.md line 73 verbatim: "Planned, not yet built: `packages/cli`,
`packages/server`, `apps/viz`, `packages/hooks`, `packages/bench`." It also
matches `blueprints/INDEX.md` rows `07-01`/`07-02`/`08-02`/`08-08`/`11-01`,
all status `pending`.

## 9. Doc inventory

`docs/Specs/` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\docs\Specs`): `GOLDEN_FIXTURE_SPEC.md`,
`Tadori-v2.1-Corrections.md`, `Tadori-v2.1-Corrections.pdf`,
`Tadori-v2.1-Frozen-Spec.pdf`.

`docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` key requirements (frozen `tadori serve` contract,
verbatim flag list from `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md:43-51`):

```text
--port <number>       Port for the local server (default: an open port).
--no-open             Do not launch a browser.
--reindex             Force a full reindex before serving.
--mode 2d             Stable default 2D interface.
--mode 2.5d           Depth-experiment mode (same data, added depth channel).
--mode 3d-experiment  Explicitly experimental free-orbit mode.
--snapshot <id>       Serve a specific stored snapshot.
```

Nine required-behavior steps are enumerated at `CLI_CONTRACT.md:19-39`
(resolve repository → load configuration → reuse/refresh snapshot → validate
→ start local API bound to `127.0.0.1` only → start visualization (2D
default) → open browser unless `--no-open` → print startup facts → stop all
child processes on Ctrl+C). Non-negotiables (`CLI_CONTRACT.md:56-62`):
localhost-only binding, invalid snapshots never served, evidence/origin/
confidence/resolution always visible, six-tool MCP interface stays separate
from `tadori serve` (must not add MCP tools). Document explicitly states
(line 3-6) it freezes the contract "before the visualization milestone
implements it... nothing here authorizes building the CLI ahead of the
Weeks 7-8 visualization phase."

`CLAUDE.md` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\CLAUDE.md`) rules: Tadori v2.1 is frozen;
use the relevant project-local skill under `.claude/skills`; golden fixtures
and schemas must not be weakened; run the validation skill after changes;
maintain `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`; do not start a later milestone without
explicit instruction; do not blindly apply global third-party skills;
project-local Tadori rules override conflicting general-purpose skill
guidance; do not push/publish/create a remote without explicit approval.

`AGENTS.md` (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\AGENTS.md`, 1 paragraph): same rule set
addressed to Codex, referencing `.agents/skills` instead of `.claude/skills`.

`IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` section list (`grep "^#"`, in file order): Current
milestone; Week 6 — Incremental indexing and hardening (complete,
2026-07-15) [with sub-sections: Migration 006 defect report; Week 6
performance evidence; Week 6 full validation]; Week 5 — Context selection and
budgeting (complete, 2026-07-14) [+ Week 5 validation]; Week 3 — Semantic
extraction (complete, 2026-07-14) [+ Week 3 validation, documented
interpretations, adversarial review outcome]; Weeks 1–2 milestone (complete);
Dual-agent configuration (Phase A — complete, 2026-07-14); Repository
environment (2026-07-14); Completed capabilities; Validation results (all
executed and observed on this machine); Fixture relations currently
supported; Relations intentionally deferred; Performance observations;
Specification deviations / documented interpretations; Discovered defects;
Known limitations (in-scope simplifications, not defects); Week 5 —
Context selection and budgeting (implementation complete, 2026-07-14)
[duplicate heading later in file]; Current roadmap phase; Repository hygiene
(2026-07-17).

Week 6 capabilities summary (`IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md:14-46`, condensed):
native repository watcher with debounced normalized batches; immutable
repository-generation capture (scan + hashes + bytes rechecked before
publication); persistent versioned TypeScript LanguageService with regional
refresh (body/dependency/test/route/ADR/barrel edits use deterministic
invalidation+merge; structural/config/analyzer-version/uncertain cases fail
closed to full rebuild); generation-CAS publication with stale overlays,
no-op and A→B→A reuse, cancellation, syntax-error rollback, crash-safe
restart reconciliation; `tadori diff .` command (base = command-start
working-tree head, falling back to commit head); production stdio runs
compiler/watcher/writer in an isolated worker so MCP reads stay responsive
(`refresh_pending`) while a replacement head builds. Performance table at
`IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md:67-75` (see also §7 above — benchmark gates match).

`README.md` current claims (`c:\SideProjects\Tadori\README.md`): Weeks 1-6 of
the frozen v2.1 roadmap complete and validated; "170/170 tests across 24
files pass"; "All five golden fixtures compare exactly"; "Incremental
refresh gates met: single-file refresh p95 1257.685 ms (< 2000 ms gate) on a
250,330-LOC corpus"; next phase is local serving and 2D visualization
(`tadori serve .`, contract in `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md`); architecture table
listing the 5 real packages + `packages/fixtures/` as fixture data (not a
workspace package); explicit "Planned, not yet built" list (line 73-74)
matching §8 above; frozen contracts section reiterating the six MCP tool
names and golden-fixture authority; roadmap pointer to `BACKLOG.md` and
`blueprints/INDEX.md`.

## 10. Hidden coupling / risks noticed

- `scan.ts`'s `classify()` (no compiler-options awareness) and `project.ts`'s two independent `allowJs`/`checkJs` gates (`project.ts:232`, `project.ts:355-356`) are three separate places that must agree about which files are "real" TypeScript-processable source; they currently do not, per §4. A fourth place, `indexRepository.ts:118-120`, re-filters `scan.indexedFiles` by `language === "typescript" || language === "javascript"` before calling `createProjectServices` — meaning the JS-vs-program-roots mismatch is actually reproduced independently in **three** call sites (`project.ts` twice, `indexRepository.ts` once via the language check that still includes `"javascript"`), not one.
- `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` explicitly forbids building `tadori serve` ahead of the Weeks 7-8 visualization phase (`CLI_CONTRACT.md:3-6`), yet the current branch is named `Sprint7-core-visualization` and `blueprints/INDEX.md` already lists Phase 7/8 blueprint rows as `pending` — any planning subagent should confirm explicit-instruction status before beginning `07-01`/`07-02` work, per `CLAUDE.md`'s "do not start a later milestone without explicit instruction."
- `blueprints/INDEX.md` row `00-01A` already tracks the exact §4 defect as `drafting`, with `blueprints/00-01A-allowjs-scanner-contract.md` presumably containing prior analysis; this evidence pack's §4 findings should be reconciled with that file rather than treated as a fresh discovery (this pack did not open that file — flagged for the next subagent).
- `packages/mcp/src/cli.ts` requires `--db`/`--repo` with no defaults, while `scripts/tadori.mts` defaults `--db` to `.tadori/tadori.sqlite` — a future unified CLI (`packages/cli`, per the roadmap) will need to reconcile these two different default-resolution behaviors, since `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` describes yet a third behavior ("resolve the repository... fail with an actionable message") for the not-yet-built `tadori serve`.
- `GraphService.open`'s fallback logic (`service.ts:137-139`) — `getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id, preferredKind) ?? (preferredKind === undefined ? undefined : getActiveSnapshot(db, repo.id))` — means a caller requesting `"working_tree"` snapshots (as `stdio.ts:31` does) will silently fall back to *any* kind (e.g. `"commit"`) if no working-tree snapshot exists yet; this is documented behavior in code but worth flagging for any HTTP-server reuse (§8) that assumes it always gets a `working_tree` snapshot.
- `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` contains two separate "Week 5 — Context selection and budgeting" headings (one at line 99 marked complete 2026-07-14, another at line 489 also marked implementation complete 2026-07-14) — likely a merge/append artifact, not a real duplication of scope, but worth a cleanup note since the file is required-maintained per `CLAUDE.md`.
- The benchmark corpus (`scripts/benchmark-incremental.mts`) generates synthetic files at "run time" under `tmpdir()` (`mkdtempSync`, imports at line 3) and is not part of the golden-fixture contract; its gates (§7) are the only quantitative performance evidence in the repo — any Phase 7+ server/serve work that adds latency should be checked against these same gates rather than inventing new ones, since `IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` and `README.md` both cite these specific numbers as current ground truth.

---

*End of evidence baseline. No production files were modified to produce
this document; only `blueprints/research/EVIDENCE-BASELINE.md` was written.*
